tagline: Speak anywhere on Windows. Murmur types it.
description: A Windows desktop dictation app with feature parity goals against Wispr Flow: press a hotkey in any app, speak, and clean formatted text is inserted at the cursor. Transcription runs on the Groq API (Whisper) by default, with any OpenAI-compatible endpoint supported for free local options. Built to be shared with coworkers: clone, npm install, add a free Groq key, done.

stack.transcription: Groq audio/transcriptions (whisper-large-v3-turbo) or any OpenAI-compatible base URL
stack.insertion: Clipboard swap + Ctrl+V via a persistent PowerShell SendKeys helper (no native build tools required)
stack.holdToTalk: uiohook-napi (prebuilt N-API binaries) with graceful fallback to toggle-only

conventions.definitionOfDone: A story passes only when every acceptance criterion is verified. Criteria that need a live microphone or a real API key are verified manually by the user and noted in `notes`.
conventions.process: Work stories in priority order. After completing a story, set passes=true, add a note describing how it was verified, and commit.

stories.title: App shell, tray, and single instance
stories.description: As a user, I launch Murmur and it lives quietly in my system tray, ready to dictate, without cluttering my taskbar.
acceptanceCriteria: npm start launches the app with no errors
Only one instance can run; launching a second focuses the existing one
A tray icon appears with a menu: Start dictation, Settings, History, Quit
Quit from the tray fully exits the process (no orphaned helpers)
A --smoke launch initializes every subsystem, prints SMOKE_RESULT JSON, and exits 0
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 via npm run smoke: all checks true, exit 0, tray created, keystroke helper spawned and disposed. Single-instance lock implemented; sanity check the double-launch focus behavior once during first live use.

stories.title: Settings persistence
stories.description: As a user, my configuration (API key, hotkeys, mic, model, toggles) persists across restarts in a local JSON file.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings are stored in %APPDATA%/murmur/settings.json
Missing or corrupt settings files fall back to defaults without crashing
Changes broadcast to open windows so the UI never shows stale values
The API key never leaves the machine except in the Authorization header to the configured endpoint
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: smoke confirms settings.json creation; corrupt-file test (garbage written to settings.json) logged a warning, fell back to defaults, and rewrote a valid file with smoke still green. Broadcast wired via the settings-changed channel. Key usage audited: only transcribe.js reads it, only into Authorization headers.

stories.title: Programmatic icon generation
stories.description: As a developer, all icons (tray idle, tray recording, app icon, installer .ico) are generated by a script so the repo ships no binary assets.
acceptanceCriteria: node scripts/gen-icons.js writes valid PNGs for tray idle, tray recording, and app icon sizes 16/32/48/256
The script writes a valid multi-size build/icon.ico consumable by electron-builder
Generation runs automatically on npm install (postinstall)
Electron nativeImage decodes the generated PNGs without error (verified in smoke test)
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: postinstall ran gen-icons.js; smoke confirms files exist and nativeImage decodes them; icon-256.png visually reviewed (amber waveform bars on ink tile); icon.ico contains 256/48/32/16 PNG entries and was consumed by electron-builder.

stories.title: Toggle hotkey and recording pipeline
stories.description: As a user, I press a global hotkey (default Ctrl+Shift+Space) in any app to start recording my voice, and press it again to stop.
acceptanceCriteria: The hotkey works while any other application has focus
Recording captures the selected microphone as webm/opus via MediaRecorder
Focus never leaves the app I was typing in (overlay is non-focusable)
Pressing Escape while recording cancels without inserting anything
Recording auto-stops at the configured max duration (default 5 minutes)
The hotkey is rebindable from Settings and re-registers live
stories.notes: Implemented: globalShortcut toggle, temporary global Esc while recording, max-duration auto-stop, live rebinding with revert + toast when registration fails, cancel-during-processing guarded by a generation counter. Needs one live run with a microphone to flip to passing.

stories.title: Dictation overlay with live waveform
stories.description: As a user, while dictating I see a small floating pill near the bottom of my screen with a live waveform, a timer, and clear state: listening, processing, inserted, or error.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay appears bottom-center of the display the cursor is on
Waveform animates from real microphone amplitude while listening
Processing state shows a distinct animation after recording stops
Success state shows the inserted word count, then the overlay hides itself
Errors show a readable message in the pill, then hide
Overlay is click-through, never steals focus, and respects prefers-reduced-motion
stories.notes: Implemented: focusable:false + setIgnoreMouseEvents + showInactive, display-nearest-cursor positioning, four states with reduced-motion fallbacks. Overlay renderer verified loading in smoke. Live waveform and state transitions need a real dictation to verify.

stories.title: Groq transcription provider
stories.description: As a user, my speech is transcribed by Groq Whisper by default, and I can point Murmur at any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (including a free local server) instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio posts to {baseUrl}/audio/transcriptions with model, optional language, and a vocabulary prompt built from my dictionary
Base URL, API key, and model are all editable in Settings
A Test connection button verifies the key against {baseUrl}/models and reports success or the exact error
Network and API errors surface as readable overlay messages (bad key, rate limit, no internet)
Requests time out rather than hanging the pipeline
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 with Labroi's real key: synthesized voice clip transcribed exactly ('Testing 1 2 3 Murmur end to end test'); Test connection returned OK in the onboarding UI; invalid key returns the friendly 401 message; whisper-large-v3-turbo confirmed live on Groq's model list; timeouts implemented via AbortSignal.

stories.title: Text insertion at the cursor
stories.description: As a user, the transcribed text is typed into whatever field my cursor was in, in any application, and my clipboard is put back the way it was.
acceptanceCriteria: Default method: clipboard swap plus a synthesized Ctrl+V, then clipboard text restore
Alternate method (Settings): literal typing via SendKeys for paste-hostile apps
SendKeys special characters are escaped so symbols, braces, and newlines insert correctly
The keystroke helper is a persistent PowerShell child process (no per-insert spawn latency, no native modules)
The helper is pingable and shuts down with the app
stories.notes: Labroi's first live dictation caught a real bug here: the helper script was joined into one line with semicolons, which breaks if/elseif in PowerShell, so every paste failed with a 'term elseif is not recognized' error (ping alone passed, masking it). Fixed 2026-07-17: script is now true multi-line via -EncodedCommand, and a new required smoke check (injectChain) exercises the full command chain. Ping, chain, and SendKeys escaping machine-verified; the paste landing in a real app still needs the next live dictation.

stories.title: Hold-to-talk
stories.description: As a user, I can hold a single key (default Right Ctrl), speak while holding, and release to insert: the signature Wispr Flow interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Holding the hold key starts recording; releasing stops and inserts
Taps shorter than 350ms cancel instead of producing an empty transcription
Key auto-repeat does not retrigger recording
The hold key is rebindable via a press-to-capture UI in Settings
If uiohook-napi fails to load, hold mode disables cleanly and toggle mode still works, with the limitation shown in Settings
stories.notes: uiohook-napi prebuilt binary loads on this machine (smoke holdAvailable=true); repeat suppression, sub-350ms tap cancel, and press-to-capture rebinding implemented; fallback path renders the limitation note in Settings. Hold/release feel needs a live keypress to verify.

stories.title: Smart formatting and custom dictionary
stories.description: As a user, my raw transcript is cleaned up (filler words removed, punctuation and casing fixed) and my custom vocabulary (names, brands, acronyms) is spelled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Smart formatting is a toggle, on by default, using a fast Groq LLM
The formatter never answers questions in the dictation; it only cleans the text
Any formatter failure falls back to the raw transcript (fail-open, never blocks insertion)
Dictionary words are managed as chips in Settings and fed to both Whisper (prompt) and the formatter
Formatted output preserves meaning; nothing is added
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 with the real key: 'um so uh what time is it you know' formatted to 'What time is it?', fillers removed, punctuation fixed, and the question was not answered. llama-3.1-8b-instant confirmed live on Groq. Fail-open guards (error, empty, or 3x-length output falls back to raw) implemented; dictionary feeds both Whisper prompt and formatter.

stories.title: Local history
stories.description: As a user, I can review my recent dictations, copy one back to the clipboard, delete items, or turn history off entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: Last 200 dictations stored locally in %APPDATA%/murmur/history.json with timestamp, word count, and duration
History tab lists items with copy and delete actions and a Clear all
A history toggle stops all writes when off
Nothing is ever sent anywhere; history is local only
stories.notes: Implemented: 200-item cap, copy/delete/clear in the History tab, historyEnabled gate checked in main before any write, live refresh via history-changed. Needs a first real dictation to watch an item flow through.

stories.title: First-run onboarding
stories.description: As a new coworker receiving this repo, the first launch walks me through the three things I need: a free Groq key, a microphone, and my first dictation.
acceptanceCriteria: First launch opens Settings with a welcome card: get key (link to console.groq.com), paste key, pick mic, try it
Test connection gives immediate feedback on the pasted key
The card never appears again after Finish (persisted flag)
Windows microphone privacy requirements are explained where mic selection happens
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17 on Labroi's first real launch: card appeared, key test showed 'OK, Connected', and the onboarded flag persisted. Found and fixed a bug where 'Start using Murmur' saved but the card stayed visible (.onboard's display:grid overrode the hidden attribute; global [hidden] { display:none !important } added). Dismissal is now a permanent smoke check (onboardDismiss).

stories.title: Polish: sounds, autostart, resilience
stories.description: As a daily user, Murmur feels finished: subtle audio cues, optional launch at login, and no weird states.
acceptanceCriteria: Subtle synthesized start/stop/error cues (WebAudio, no audio assets), toggleable
Launch at login toggle wired to Windows login items
Tray icon reflects recording state
Hotkeys ignored while a dictation is already processing (no re-entrancy)
Overlay positions correctly on multi-monitor setups (display nearest cursor)
stories.notes: Implemented: WebAudio blips (no assets), setLoginItemSettings wiring, tray icon swap on record, idle/listening/processing state machine blocks re-entrancy, getDisplayNearestPoint positioning. Verify feel during first live session.

stories.title: Packaging and coworker docs
stories.description: As a coworker, I can either run from source with two commands or be handed a single portable .exe, and the README answers every setup question.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dist produces a portable .exe and an NSIS installer via electron-builder (npmRebuild disabled so no build tools are required)
README covers: quick start, free Groq key steps, hotkeys, all settings, costs, free/local alternatives, privacy, troubleshooting
Repo contains no secrets, no binaries, and a proper .gitignore
MIT licensed so sharing internally is frictionless
stories.notes: Verified 2026-07-17: npm run dist produced release/Murmur-0.1.0-portable.exe (85.6 MB) and Murmur Setup 0.1.0.exe with npmRebuild off. README covers quick start, key steps, hotkeys, costs, local/free alternatives, privacy, troubleshooting. .gitignore excludes node_modules, release, generated assets; MIT LICENSE added; no secrets in repo.
